methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Mytra, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Chris Walti, has raised over $200M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers, including 8 private companies (Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, and others) and 1 public company, reflecting the capital intensity and relative immaturity of the robotics sector.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $200M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, yields $340M. Peer-median analysis across the 9-company comparable set—with valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B—produces a $1.6B midpoint, anchored by recent Series C and B transactions (Hadrian at $1.6B, 1X Technologies at $10.0B, Exotec at $2.0B). The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity, deployment stage, and market positioning within the robotics space.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $289M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $970M. The stage-progression method pulls toward the lower end, while peer comparables cluster significantly higher. Key uncertainties include Mytra's capital efficiency relative to peers, commercialization progress, and whether the peer set's wide range ($300M–$10.0B) adequately reflects Mytra's specific technical and market positioning within manufacturing robotics.
